title: Hierarchical porous coating for passive daytime radiative cooling
abstract: We report a scalable polymer-ceramic coating that combines high solar reflectance with strong mid-infrared thermal emission through the atmospheric window. The study quantifies spectral emissivity, outdoor cooling power, and radiative heat-transfer limits under humid conditions.
keywords: passive daytime radiative cooling; thermal emission; emissivity
rationale: Radiative cooling and thermal emission are central.

title: Near-field thermal radiation between patterned silicon carbide gratings
abstract: This paper analyzes near-field thermal radiation across nanoscale vacuum gaps between patterned SiC gratings. Fluctuational electrodynamics simulations show how surface phonon polariton coupling modifies heat flux and spectral transfer.
keywords: near-field thermal radiation; surface phonon polariton; vacuum gap
rationale: Near-field thermal radiation is the main mechanism.

title: Tunable selective thermal emitter for thermophotovoltaic conversion
abstract: We demonstrate a refractory nanophotonic selective emitter with temperature-stable emissivity peaks matched to a thermophotovoltaic cell bandgap. The contribution is spectral control of thermal radiation from high-temperature surfaces.
rationale: TPV selective thermal emission is central.

title: Spectral emissivity measurement of high-temperature ceramic coatings
abstract: A calibrated blackbody reference and infrared spectrometer are used to measure temperature-dependent spectral emissivity of ceramic coatings from 700 to 1400 K. The paper evaluates uncertainty in thermal radiation metrology for high-temperature materials.
keywords: spectral emissivity; blackbody; thermal radiation metrology
rationale: Emissivity and blackbody metrology are central.

title: Radiative transfer model for packed nanoparticle insulation
abstract: We develop a radiative transfer model for participating media in packed nanoparticle beds and validate it against infrared transmittance measurements. The work identifies size-dependent absorption and scattering effects on thermal radiation transport.
keywords: radiative transfer; participating media; thermal insulation
rationale: Radiative transfer is the main technical contribution.

title: Mid-infrared photonic crystal thermal emitters with angular selectivity
abstract: The study designs and fabricates photonic crystal thermal emitters that control mid-infrared emission angle and polarization. Measurements of emissivity spectra confirm selective thermal emission beyond blackbody-like behavior.
rationale: Thermal emitter design and emissivity control are central.

title: Adaptive radiative cooling film based on vanadium dioxide
abstract: We present a VO2-based adaptive radiative cooling film whose infrared emissivity changes across the phase transition. Outdoor tests and radiative heat-transfer modeling quantify cooling and heat-retention behavior.
rationale: Adaptive emissivity for radiative cooling is central.

title: Solar-thermal selective absorber with suppressed infrared emission
abstract: A multilayer solar-thermal selective absorber is optimized to maximize solar absorption while reducing mid-infrared thermal emission at operating temperature. The paper reports absorptivity, emissivity, and radiative loss analysis.
rationale: Selective absorber/radiative loss analysis is central.

title: Hyperbolic metamaterial enhancement of near-field radiative heat transfer
abstract: The manuscript quantifies near-field radiative heat transfer between hyperbolic metamaterial slabs separated by a sub-100-nm vacuum gap. It compares modal density and spectral heat flux using fluctuational electrodynamics.
keywords: near-field radiative heat transfer; hyperbolic metamaterial; thermal radiation
rationale: Near-field radiative heat transfer is central.

title: Planck-limited thermal emission from graphene-tuned metasurfaces
abstract: We examine thermal emission from graphene-tuned metasurfaces and compare measured spectra with Planck-law predictions. The core result is electrical control of emissivity in the mid-infrared.
rationale: Thermal emission and emissivity control are central.

title: Radiation heat-transfer limits in high-temperature vacuum insulation
abstract: The paper derives radiation heat-transfer limits for multilayer high-temperature vacuum insulation and tests predictions using guarded hot-plate measurements. Conduction is minimized so that thermal radiation dominates heat exchange.
keywords: radiation heat transfer; vacuum insulation; thermal radiation
rationale: Radiation-dominated heat transfer is central.

title: Infrared thermal camouflage using wavelength-selective emitters
abstract: We design wavelength-selective thermal emitters for infrared thermal camouflage and quantify their apparent temperature under varying backgrounds. The study centers on engineered emissivity and spectral thermal radiation control.
keywords: thermal emitter; infrared; emissivity; spectral selectivity
rationale: Engineered thermal emission is central.

title: Deep learning for radiation oncology dose planning in lung tumors
abstract: A neural network is trained to predict dose distributions for radiotherapy treatment planning in lung tumor patients. The study evaluates clinical dose-volume metrics and treatment delivery constraints.
rationale: Biomedical ionizing-radiation use of radiation.

title: Gamma radiation shielding performance of polymer composites
abstract: This work measures gamma-ray attenuation coefficients of polymer composites for nuclear shielding. The analysis focuses on ionizing radiation protection and mass attenuation at several photon energies.
rationale: Nuclear/ionizing radiation, not thermal radiation.

title: Wireless electromagnetic radiation exposure near 5G base stations
abstract: The manuscript maps radiofrequency electromagnetic radiation levels near 5G antennas and compares measured fields with public exposure limits. It does not study heat transfer or thermal emission.
rationale: Wireless exposure topic, not thermal-radiation science.

title: Satellite remote sensing of land surface temperature in arid regions
abstract: The paper uses satellite thermal bands to estimate land surface temperature and drought indices over an arid region. The contribution is geospatial remote sensing workflow validation rather than thermal-radiation physics or device design.
keywords: remote sensing; land surface temperature; satellite
rationale: Remote sensing application without thermal-radiation contribution.

title: Transformer model for solar power output forecasting
abstract: We propose a transformer architecture for day-ahead solar power forecasting using weather, irradiance, and historical generation data. The work evaluates prediction accuracy for grid operation.
keywords: solar power forecasting; machine learning; grid operation
rationale: Generic forecasting, not thermal-radiation contribution.

title: CFD study of battery thermal management with liquid cooling
abstract: A computational fluid dynamics model is used to optimize liquid-channel geometry for battery thermal management. Heat transfer is dominated by convection and conduction; radiation is neglected because operating temperatures are low.
rationale: Battery CFD with radiation absent/neglected.

title: X-ray imaging dose reduction using sparse-view reconstruction
abstract: The study introduces an image reconstruction algorithm for reducing X-ray CT dose in medical imaging. Evaluation uses anatomical phantoms and clinical image-quality metrics.
rationale: Medical X-ray imaging is not thermal radiation.

title: Cosmic radiation risk assessment for long-duration spaceflight
abstract: This paper estimates astronaut exposure to galactic cosmic radiation during long-duration spaceflight and evaluates shielding strategies. The focus is particle radiation risk and biology.
rationale: Cosmic particle radiation, not thermal radiation.

title: UV radiation damage in polymer outdoor aging
abstract: We investigate ultraviolet radiation damage in outdoor-aged polymer films using mechanical testing and chemical spectroscopy. Thermal emission and radiative heat transfer are not addressed.
rationale: UV photodegradation rather than thermal radiation.

title: Information radiator dashboard for agile software teams
abstract: The paper evaluates an information radiator dashboard for agile project management and team communication. It reports survey results from software development teams.
rationale: Nonphysical use of radiator/radiation vocabulary.

title: Radiofrequency antenna design for low-power IoT devices
abstract: A compact antenna geometry is proposed for low-power wireless IoT devices. The study optimizes gain, bandwidth, and electromagnetic radiation patterns for communication.
rationale: Wireless EM radiation, not thermal radiation.

title: Neutron transport simulation in compact nuclear reactors
abstract: The manuscript develops a neutron transport solver for compact nuclear reactor analysis. It benchmarks multiplication factors and shielding dose predictions against reference calculations.
rationale: Nuclear neutron transport is outside thermal-radiation scope.

title: Battery pack thermal management including surface-to-surface radiation
abstract: A coupled CFD model predicts battery pack temperature under fast charging. The model includes surface-to-surface radiation among modules, but the proposed design change is a liquid-cooling channel layout and the radiation contribution is not isolated.
keywords: battery thermal management; CFD; surface radiation
rationale: Radiation appears as a component, but not central novelty.

title: Industrial furnace optimization with coupled convection and radiation
abstract: The study optimizes an industrial furnace operating schedule using a coupled convection-radiation heat-transfer model. The main output is reduced fuel cost, while the thermal radiation model uses a standard gray-gas approximation.
rationale: Radiation is relevant but embedded in process optimization.

title: Building envelope design with radiative cooling coating and HVAC savings
abstract: A building energy simulation evaluates roof coatings, insulation thickness, and HVAC control strategies. One option uses a radiative cooling coating, but the manuscript focuses on annual energy savings rather than thermal-radiation mechanisms.
keywords: building envelope; radiative cooling; energy simulation
rationale: Radiative cooling is one intervention inside building-energy analysis.

title: Combustion chamber wall-temperature prediction with participating-media radiation
abstract: A CFD solver predicts wall temperature in a combustion chamber using turbulence, convection, and participating-media radiation models. The paper validates wall temperatures but does not introduce a new radiation model or measurement.
rationale: Radiation is modeled but not the clear contribution.

title: Solar receiver thermal losses under wind and radiative exchange
abstract: The manuscript analyzes heat loss from a solar receiver under varying wind conditions. It includes convection and radiative exchange calculations, but the design conclusions are mainly driven by wind shielding and receiver geometry.
keywords: solar receiver; thermal losses; radiative exchange
rationale: Thermal radiation matters but may not be the central novelty.

title: Radiative panels for electronics cooling in mixed convection environments
abstract: The paper tests panels with high infrared emissivity for electronics cooling, alongside fans and heat sinks. The results suggest radiative heat transfer can reduce peak temperature, but convection remains the dominant mode.
keywords: electronics cooling; emissivity; convection; radiative heat transfer
rationale: Strong mixed-mode heat-transfer case requiring editor judgment.

title: A novel thermal radiation method for energy systems
abstract: We propose a novel method for energy systems with broad applications. The approach improves performance compared with existing methods and may support future sustainable technologies.
keywords: thermal radiation; energy systems; novel method
rationale: Too vague despite scope words.

title: AI-assisted analysis of heat and radiation
abstract: This paper presents an AI-assisted analysis of heat and radiation. The framework is general and can be applied to many engineering problems with improved performance.
rationale: No mechanism, domain, or contribution specified.

title: Advanced materials for sustainable thermal control
abstract: Advanced materials are developed for sustainable thermal control in next-generation devices. The manuscript reports improved performance and broad applications in energy and environmental systems.
rationale: No evidence thermal radiation is central.

title: Radiation-aware design of next-generation devices
abstract: We introduce radiation-aware design principles for next-generation devices and validate the strategy through simulations. The abstract does not specify the radiation type, temperature range, or physical mechanism.
rationale: Radiation type and method unclear.

title: New framework for thermal performance enhancement
abstract: A new framework is proposed for thermal performance enhancement. The model is accurate, efficient, and applicable to a wide range of systems.
rationale: Too short and generic.

title: Multiphysics optimization of materials under radiation
abstract: The study performs multiphysics optimization of materials under radiation exposure. Results demonstrate improved robustness, but the abstract does not state whether radiation refers to thermal emission, ionizing exposure, or another mechanism.
rationale: Ambiguous radiation sense.
